docs.title: Start
docs.text:
Eddy3D is a Grasshopper plugin for Rhino 8 for urban microclimate and airflow simulation. Everything now ships in a single Eddy3D package that bundles several modules:

  • Eddy3D Outdoor

    Decoupled microclimate simulations, via wind and mean radiant temperature simulations. Driven by OpenFOAM and Radiance.

    Outdoor

  • Eddy3D Outdoor+

    Fully coupled microclimate simulations, including wind, radiation, heat, and moisture transfer, driven by urbanMicroclimateFoam by ETH Zurich Building Physics.

    Outdoor+

  • Eddy3D Indoor

    Modeling airflow, moisture content, and passive scalars in indoor spaces.

    Indoor

docs.title: Plugin
docs.text: Install Eddy3D via the Rhino Package Manager (yak) on either platform — run PackageManager in Rhino and search for Eddy3D.

docs.title: Simulation Engines (Windows / Mac)
docs.text:
Choose a simulation engine (one of the following):

  • Docker: Download Docker Desktop (Recommended cross-platform, pulls a pre-configured OpenFOAM 12 image automatically)
  • BlueCFD-Core 2024 (Windows Only): Download blueCFD-Core
  • WSL (Windows Only): WSL Installation guide for Windows (Requires urbanMicroclimateFoam to be installed manually)

The MRT component uses Radiance. The Install Engines component downloads and installs it automatically (Windows & Mac, release rad6R0P2) under the per-user Eddy3D folder — no manual install needed.

docs.title: Downloads
docs.text:
Install Eddy3D from the Rhino Package Manager (run PackageManager in Rhino 8 and search for Eddy3D). For the current version and release notes, see the Eddy3D download page.

Previous Versions

View the required Rhino 8.27 build details (8.27.26019.16022, en-us).

One package

All modules — Outdoor, Outdoor+, Indoor, MRT, and FluidX3D — now ship in the single Eddy3D package on the Rhino Package Manager (yak). The former separate UMCF package (Outdoor+) has been retired.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Start from a template

Eddy3D ships with starter templates for every module. Right-click the Template component in Grasshopper to browse and load one, then follow the numbered markers through the canvas.

docs.title: Modules
docs.text:
Outdoor — Wind & MRT

Decoupled microclimate simulations via wind (OpenFOAM) and mean radiant temperature (Radiance).

Outdoor+ — Coupled microclimate

Outdoor+ is the fully coupled microclimate module of the Eddy3D plugin, built on the open-source urbanMicroclimateFoam (UMCF) solver for OpenFOAM.

Indoor — Airflow

Modeling airflow, moisture content, and passive scalars in indoor spaces.

See the Video Tutorials page for the Indoor Wind & COVID-19 walkthrough.

docs.title: Parallel computation
docs.text: There is currently an issue with Microsoft’s and BlueCFD’s MPI dll which is why a run with multiple CPUs might fail. You need both dlls to be the same file, see the CFD Online instructions for matching MPI DLL files to ensure that both DLLs are the same file.

docs.title: Simple workflows
docs.text:
We value efficient workflows! See below for a one-directional urban CFD setup.

Video walkthroughs are on the Video Tutorials page.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
🌊 CFD - Solves turbulent, convective airflow

  • Handles heat and moisture transport in the air subdomain

🏗️ HAM - Manages absorption and transport

  • Controls storage of heat and moisture in porous building materials

☀️ RAD - Calculates net longwave and shortwave radiative heat fluxes

  • Uses view factor approach

🌳 VEG - Solves heat balance for urban trees

  • Handles green surfaces

docs.title: Acknowledgments
docs.text:
Outdoor+ is based on the urbanMicroclimateFoam (UMF) open-source solver based on OpenFOAM, developed by the Chair of Building Physics at ETH Zürich the Canada Research Chair Tier I in Multiscale Building Physics at Université de Sherbrooke, Canada.

  • urbanMicroclimateFoam GitHub repository

This project is partially funded by Perkins&Will Research. Their support has been instrumental in advancing this tool.

This project originated from the VIP - Surrogate Models for Urban Regeneration during Fall 2024. Visit the VIP - Surrogate Models for Urban Regeneration documentation for more details and view the final presentation.

docs.title: Eddy3D - Outdoor+
docs.text: The urban microclimate module of the Eddy3D plugin, using urbanMicroclimateFoam (uMFoam)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
urbanMicroclimateFoam (uMFoam) is an open-source solver built on OpenFOAM for modeling urban microclimates. It simulates coupled physical processes including:

  • Turbulent airflow
  • Heat and moisture transport in air
  • Radiative heat exchange (shortwave and longwave)
  • Heat and moisture storage in building materials (HAM model)
  • Urban vegetation heat balance

Learn more at the official repository: 🔗 urbanMicroclimateFoam GitHub

docs.title: 1. Install an OpenFOAM engine
docs.text:
Eddy3D runs OpenFOAM through one of these engines (pick one):

  • Docker (Windows & macOS, recommended): Install Docker Desktop. Eddy3D pulls a pre-configured OpenFOAM 12 image automatically.
  • blueCFD-Core 2024 (Windows): Download blueCFD-Core.
  • WSL (Windows): WSL installation guide.

docs.title: 2. Install the Eddy3D Plugin for Grasshopper (Rhino)
docs.text:
• Open Rhinoceros
  • Run the PackageManager command
  • Search for Eddy3D
  • Click Install and restart Rhino

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
Use the Check Installation component in Grasshopper:

  • Set Check = True
  • Set Install UrbanMicroclimateFoam = True if it’s a new install

The console will automatically install the necessary files.

docs.title: 1. Load Template
docs.text:
• Use the Templates component
  • Right-click > select UMCF.Templates.MicroclimateSimulation.gh

docs.title: 2. Add Weather File
docs.text:
• Connect an EPW file to the Weather component
  • This enables time settings in the Timing component

docs.title: Air Region
docs.text:
Use the Air Region component to set:

  • ABL Conditions: Wind speed, height, direction
  • Domain Dimensions:
  • Cell Size
  • Front Add, Back Add, Sides Add, Top Add
  • Refinement Box Add

docs.title: Solid Region (Buildings)
docs.text:
Inputs:

  • Building mesh geometry
  • Material (default: Hamstad5Brick)
  • Mesh refinement (optional)
  • Indoor temperature (°C)

docs.title: Vegetation Region
docs.text:
Inputs:

  • Vegetation mesh
  • Leaf Area Density (LAD)
  • Vegetation properties
  • Mesh refinement (optional)

Default vegetation values are found in constant/air/vegetationProperties.

docs.title: Simulation Settings
docs.text:
Configure with the Simulation Settings component:

  • Number of CPUs (e.g., 10 recommended)
  • maxFluidIteration (higher = better resolution)

docs.title: Write and Run the Case
docs.text:
Use the UMCF Case component to prepare files and view logs.

  1. Prepare Simulation – generate mesh
  2. Run Simulation – execute solver

Run both steps with the same processor mode (single/parallel).

After simulation: - View results with .foam in ParaView - 0 folder = initial conditions - Output folders based on controlDict: - startTime = 0, endTime = 43200, deltaT = 3600 (i.e., 12 hours)

docs.title: Visualize Results (in Rhino/Grasshopper)
docs.text:
Use a quad mesh plane at desired height in the Probing section.

Probe for: - Temperature (T) - Velocity (U) - Humidity (w)

Steps:

  1. Generate mesh plane
  2. Click Run
  3. Enable Probing to load results
  4. Use Hour Slider to view time-based results

docs.title: Output Types
docs.text:
• Velocity (U)
  • Temperature (T)
  • Humidity (w)

Visualizations are available both in Grasshopper and ParaView.

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text: Visit the UMCF GitHub Issues page or reach out to your instructor/supervisor for support.

docs.title: Atmospheric Boundary Layer - [source code]
docs.text: Define atmospheric boundary layer inflow conditions for Eddy3D.

docs.title: Dirs
docs.text: Wind directions as meteorological degrees (wind-from, clockwise from north) or flow vectors. One solver case is created per direction. Optional; default is flow toward +X.

docs.title: Wind Speed (U)
docs.text: Wind speed at the reference height (m/s), one value per wind direction. A single value applies to all directions; a shorter list repeats its last value. Optional; default is 5.

docs.title: Zref
docs.text: Reference height for wind speed (m). Optional; default is 10.

docs.title: z0
docs.text: Aerodynamic roughness length (m). Higher values indicate rougher terrain. Optional; default is 1.

docs.title: Zgnd
docs.text: Ground/displacement height for the ABL log-law profile (m). Optional; default is 0.

docs.title: k
docs.text: Inlet/initial turbulent kinetic energy k (m^2/s^2) for the ABL. Used by the k field and the turbulence transports; the inlet patch still uses the atmBoundaryLayer k profile. Optional; default is 0.015.

docs.title: epsilon
docs.text: Inlet/initial turbulent dissipation rate epsilon (m^2/s^3) for the k-epsilon family. The inlet patch still uses the atmBoundaryLayer epsilon profile. Optional; default is 0.135.

docs.title: omega
docs.text: Inlet/initial specific dissipation rate omega (1/s) for the k-omega SST model. Optional; default is 100.

docs.title: Boundary Conditions (BC)
docs.text: Atmospheric boundary layer inflow boundary conditions (including the wind directions); plug into the wind case BC input.

docs.title: Vectors
docs.text: Resolved unit flow vectors, one per wind direction.

docs.title: Cylinder Domain - [source code]
docs.text: Define a cylindrical simulation domain for Eddy3D. One cylindrical mesh serves all wind directions; the cylinder side faces switch between inlet and outlet per direction.

docs.title: Radius
docs.text: Outer radius of the cylindrical domain in meters. -1 = auto from the building height.

docs.title: Height
docs.text: Height of the cylindrical domain in meters. -1 = auto from the building height.

docs.title: RadMult
docs.text: Cell growth factor from the core toward the perimeter. Default: 2.

docs.title: Divs
docs.text: Cells per core block (and per perimeter segment, tangentially). Refines the core without changing the block layout that Core Cell Size sets; the preview core grid densifies to match. Default: 1.

docs.title: Refine
docs.text: Padding of the refinement box around the geometry (m). -1 = auto: 27.5% of the building footprint.

docs.title: Grade
docs.text: Far-field cell coarsening across the perimeter ring (outer cell size / inner cell size). 1 = uniform; >1 keeps fine cells at the buildings but grows the outer cells. The default 7 coarsens the far field aggressively (far fewer cells downwind); lower it (1-3) for a gentler, more uniform ring. Default: 7.

docs.title: Round
docs.text: Rounds the O-grid inner core from a square (0) toward a circle (1). Higher values even out the radial gap to the outer boundary, cutting the corner non-orthogonality of the square core. 0 keeps the classic square core. 0.65 is the checkMesh-sweep optimum (lowest max non-orthogonality across mesh resolutions; skewness stays well within limits). Default: 0.65.

docs.title: GradeZ
docs.text: Vertical cell expansion ratio (top cell size / bottom cell size). 1 = uniform; >1 keeps fine cells near the ground and coarsens aloft (typical for an ABL). A checkMesh sweep showed non-orthogonality/skewness are unaffected by this; the only cost is background aspect ratio. The default 35 strongly refines the near-ground layer; ease it down if convergence struggles. Default: 35.

docs.title: Run Settings - [source code]
docs.text: Configure solver run controls for Eddy3D.

docs.title: Iter
docs.text: Total solver iterations (endTime). Optional; default is 1000.

docs.title: WriteInt
docs.text: Write interval in iterations. Optional; default is 20.

docs.title: Keep
docs.text: Number of time directories to keep (purgeWrite). Optional; default is 3.

docs.title: Turb
docs.text: RANS turbulence model (OpenFOAM 12). The four best for urban wind: Realizable k-ε (default; AIJ/COST732 best practice for pedestrian wind), Standard k-ε (ABL baseline), RNG k-ε (high-strain / recirculation), and SST k-ω (separation, near-wall). All run on the template's existing fields.

docs.title: CPU
docs.text: Number of CPUs/subdomains for parallel runs. -1 (default) = auto: all cores but one. Optional.

docs.title: Algo
docs.text: Pressure-velocity coupling for the steady solver. SIMPLE (default) is the robust, conservatively under-relaxed baseline. SIMPLEC adds the consistent correction so it tolerates much higher relaxation (p 0.7, U/turbulence 0.9), which usually converges in fewer iterations on a good urban mesh — try it for speed, fall back to SIMPLE if a hard case stalls or oscillates. Optional; default is SIMPLE.

docs.title: Age
docs.text: Solve the mean age of air (OpenFOAM 'age' function object) during the run. Age [s] is the time since the air at each point entered the domain — the urban ventilation/stagnation map (low = well flushed, high = trapped in canyons/courtyards). Adds a cheap scalar transport solved at each write; the 'age' field is then viewable in ParaView or probe-able. Optional; default is false.

docs.title: Robust
docs.text: Numerics on a 1-5 accuracy<->robustness dial that sets the convection schemes, under-relaxation factors and non-orthogonal correctors together. 1 = most accurate (second-order convection, high relaxation) — captures wakes/eddies but needs a good mesh; 5 = most robust (first-order upwind, heavy relaxation) — always converges but smears wakes by numerical diffusion; 3 = balanced TVD. 4 reproduces the shipped baseline, so the default changes nothing. Pairs with the algorithm: SIMPLEC tolerates the higher-relaxation accurate levels (1-2) best. Optional; default is 4.

docs.title: PotFoam
docs.text: Initialise velocity/pressure with a quick potentialFoam (potential-flow Laplace) solve before simpleFoam, so the run starts from a developed, divergence-free field instead of a uniform one. Removes the first-iteration velocity overshoot of a cold start and shortens convergence. Complements the Warm-up Iterations ramp (potentialFoam seeds the field; the ramp damps the first iterations with first-order upwind + heavy turbulence relaxation). Optional; default is off (shipped behaviour).

docs.title: Warmup
docs.text: Run this many initial iterations with robust 1st-order upwind + heavy turbulence under-relaxation, then ramp up to the numerics dial for the rest of the run. Tames the cold-start velocity overshoot without losing final accuracy. -1 (default) = automatic: scales with the robustness dial (robust levels 4-5 warm up, accurate levels 1-2 do not). 0 = force off. >0 = explicit count.

docs.title: Monitors
docs.text: Write the domain min/max-magnitude and volume-average of the solved fields (U, p, k, ε/ω, νt) each write-time. Cheap convergence + stability monitor — the max |U| trace flags a cold-start overshoot or divergence early. Default off.

docs.title: PedAvg
docs.text: Write the area-average of U on a horizontal plane at pedestrian height (1.75 m) each write — the comfort-relevant mean wind speed, sampled from the live mesh (no topoSet). Default off.

docs.title: Cp
docs.text: Write the surface pressure coefficient (Cp) field each write, computed from the ABL wind speed at building height (dynamic pressure reference). View it on the building surfaces in ParaView. Default off.

docs.title: Conv
docs.text: Auto-stop tolerance: the solver stops once the p, U and turbulence initial residuals all drop below this value (SIMPLE residualControl) — typically 30-60 % faster than running the full iteration count. Default 1e-4; 0 disables auto-stop (always runs all iterations).

docs.text: Compute a thermal comfort metric at a point: UTCI (Ta, RH, wind, MRT), PET (adds the personal inputs), or NOAA Heat Index (Ta, RH only). Pick the metric from the dropdown — the inputs adapt. Wire hourly lists (e.g. EPW series) to compute annual values.

docs.title: Metric
docs.text: Comfort metric to compute; the inputs adapt to the choice.

docs.title: Ta
docs.text: Air temperature [°C].

docs.title: Relative Humidity (RH)
docs.text: Relative humidity [%].

docs.title: Wind
docs.text: Wind speed at the subject [m/s].

docs.title: MRT
docs.text: Mean radiant temperature [°C].

docs.title: Value
docs.text: Metric value [°C].

docs.title: Stress
docs.text: Thermal stress category.

docs.title: Details
docs.text: Calculation details.

docs.title: Timing Parameters - [source code]
docs.text: Define simulation timing and optional weather-driven time series. OutdoorPlus

docs.title: Weather
docs.text: Optional Weather object or EPW file path for weather-driven time series.

docs.title: StartDay
docs.text: Day-of-year index to start (0-based). Optional; default is 0.

docs.title: StartHour
docs.text: Hour of day to start (0-23). Optional; default is 0.

docs.title: Hours
docs.text: Simulation duration in hours. Optional; default is 24.

docs.title: Timing
docs.text: Simulation timing settings.

docs.title: Translate Date To Hours - [source code]
docs.text: Translate a Ladybug analysis period to hours of the year.

docs.title: Period
docs.text: Analysis period (Ladybug format), e.g. (2,2,2) and (11,11,11) for the second hour of February 2nd until the 11th hour of November 11th.

docs.title: Hours
docs.text: 1-based hours of the year inside the analysis period.

docs.title: Tree - [source code]
docs.text: Represents a tree as a porous zone for wind blocking (Darcy-Forchheimer). Feed into the wind case component.

docs.title: Geo
docs.text: Tree/vegetation geometry (one per tree for correct sizing).

docs.title: Type
docs.text: Tree density type: 'coarse', 'medium', or 'dense'. Or custom Darcy-Forchheimer B and A coefficient triples on two lines.

docs.title: LAI
docs.text: Leaf Area Index. Typical: 2 (sparse) to 6 (dense). Alternative to Type.

docs.title: Trees
docs.text: Tree porous-zone object; plug into the wind case Trees input.

docs.title: Tree Settings - [source code]
docs.text: Canopy material properties for an MRT tree surface.

docs.title: Reflectivity (R)
docs.text: Canopy shortwave reflectivity 0–1.

docs.title: Emissivity (E)
docs.text: Canopy longwave emissivity 0–1.

docs.title: RadMat
docs.text: Optional custom Radiance material string for the tree canopy.

docs.title: Mat
docs.text: Tree/canopy material for the MRT Surface component's Material input.

docs.title: UTCI (Simulation) - [source code]
docs.text: Compute annual per-probe UTCI from simulation outputs: MRT and wind-speed data trees, plus air temperature and relative humidity. For a weather-only calculator, use "UTCI (Weather)".

docs.title: MRT (M)
docs.text: DataTree of annual MRT values {probe}(8760). Each branch = one probe.

docs.title: Wind Speed (V)
docs.text: Annual per-probe wind field from the Wind Factors component (its Wind Speed output). Pedestrian-height speeds are lifted to the 10 m UTCI reference. Defaults to calm (0.5 m/s) when not connected.

docs.title: Air Temperature (T)
docs.text: Annual hourly ambient air temperature (8760 values, °C).

docs.title: Relative Humidity (RH)
docs.text: Annual hourly relative humidity (8760 values, %).

docs.title: UTCI (U)
docs.text: DataTree {probe}(8760 hourly UTCI values).

docs.title: Comfort Hours (H)
docs.text: No-thermal-stress hours per probe.

docs.title: Comfort % (%)
docs.text: Comfort percentage per probe.

docs.title: UTCI (Weather) - [source code]
docs.text: Universal Thermal Climate Index from weather inputs. Connect an EPW (supplies wind, RH, ambient temp) and/or override Ambient Temp, RH, Wind, and MRT by hand. Item or list.

docs.title: EPW (W)
docs.text: Path to an EPW weather file (optional). When connected, supplies Wind, Relative Humidity, and Ambient Temperature for any of those left unconnected. Manual inputs override the EPW.

docs.title: MRT (M)
docs.text: Mean radiant temperature (°C), as a single value or an hourly list. In EPW mode, MRT defaults to the ambient (dry-bulb) air temperature when not connected.

docs.title: Ambient Temp (T)
docs.text: Ambient (dry-bulb) air temperature (°C), value or list. Overrides the EPW; required in manual mode (no EPW).

docs.title: Relative Humidity (RH)
docs.text: Relative humidity (%), value or list. Overrides the EPW; required in manual mode.

docs.title: Wind Speed (V)
docs.text: Wind speed at the 10 m UTCI reference height (m/s), value or list. Overrides the EPW; required in manual mode.

docs.title: UTCI (U)
docs.text: Universal Thermal Climate Index (°C equivalent).

docs.title: Stress (S)
docs.text: Thermal-stress category: 0 = no stress, negative = cold stress, positive = heat stress.

docs.title: Category (C)
docs.text: Thermal-stress category name.

docs.title: Comfort Hours (H)
docs.text: No-thermal-stress hours (UTCI category 0). Emitted only when a full 8760-hour series is given.

docs.title: Comfort % (%)
docs.text: Percentage of comfortable hours over the year. Emitted only when a full 8760-hour series is given.

docs.title: Uniform Flow - [source code]
docs.text: Create a uniform (constant velocity) inflow boundary condition for Eddy3D.

docs.title: Dirs
docs.text: Wind directions as meteorological degrees (wind-from, clockwise from north) or flow vectors. One solver case is created per direction. Optional; default is flow toward +X.

docs.title: Wind Speed (U)
docs.text: Uniform inflow speed (m/s), one value per wind direction. A single value applies to all directions; a shorter list repeats its last value. Optional; default is 5.

docs.title: z0
docs.text: Aerodynamic roughness length (m), recorded for roughness-aware turbulence wall functions. Does not change the uniform inlet velocity. Optional; default is 1.

docs.title: Boundary Conditions (BC)
docs.text: Uniform inflow boundary conditions (including the wind directions); plug into the wind case BC input.

docs.title: Vectors
docs.text: Resolved unit flow vectors, one per wind direction.

docs.title: Uniform Value - [source code]
docs.text: Create a uniform scalar Value.

docs.title: Value
docs.text: Scalar value to assign.

docs.title: Value
docs.text: Uniform Value instance.

docs.title: Uniform Vector Value - [source code]
docs.text: Create a uniform vector Value.

docs.title: Vec
docs.text: Vector value to assign.

docs.title: Value
docs.text: Uniform Vector Value instance.

docs.title: Vegetation Mesh Settings - [source code]
docs.text: Configure mesh refinement for vegetation regions. OutdoorPlus

docs.title: Feature Refinement (FR)
docs.text: Feature refinement level. Optional; default is 2.

docs.title: MnSR
docs.text: Minimum refinement on surfaces. Optional; default is 4.

docs.title: MxSR
docs.text: Maximum refinement on surfaces. Optional; default is 5.

docs.title: Mesh
docs.text: Mesh refinement settings for vegetation regions.

docs.title: Vegetation Properties - [source code]
docs.text: Define vegetation property coefficients for canopy modeling. OutdoorPlus

docs.title: Cd
docs.text: Vegetation drag coefficient (Cd). Used in momentum sink and turbulence model.

docs.title: Aerodynamic Resistance (C) (C)
docs.text: Proportionality factor for aerodynamic resistance calculation.

docs.title: l
docs.text: Characteristic leaf length (l) for aerodynamic resistance.

docs.title: rsMin
docs.text: Minimum stomatal resistance (rsMin).

docs.title: EvapSides
docs.text: Number of evaporation sides for transpiration calculation.

docs.title: kc
docs.text: Radiation extinction coefficient (kc).

docs.title: Props
docs.text: Vegetation properties as a Setting instance.

docs.title: Vegetation Region - [source code]
docs.text: Create a vegetation region with properties and mesh settings. OutdoorPlus

docs.title: Mesh
docs.text: Vegetation meshes for the region.

docs.title: LAD
docs.text: Leaf area density for vegetation.

docs.title: Props
docs.text: Optional vegetation property settings.

docs.title: MeshSet
docs.text: Optional meshing settings for vegetation.

docs.title: Vegetation
docs.text: Vegetation region object for the case.

docs.title: Vegetation Settings - [source code]
docs.text: Leaf/canopy material properties for an MRT vegetation surface.

docs.title: Height Plants (HP)
docs.text: Height of plants (m).

docs.title: LAI
docs.text: Leaf area index (dimensionless).

docs.title: LeafReflectivity (LR)
docs.text: Leaf reflectivity 0–1.

docs.title: LeafEmissivity (LE)
docs.text: Leaf emissivity 0–1.

docs.title: MSR
docs.text: Minimum stomatal resistance (s/m).

docs.title: RadMat
docs.text: Optional custom Radiance material string.

docs.title: Mat
docs.text: Vegetation material for the MRT Surface component's Material input.

docs.title: Velocity Amplification Factors (VAF) - [source code]
docs.text: Compute Velocity Amplification Factors (VAF) and annual wind speed at probes from CFD results and EPW weather data. VAF (the term used in the wind-engineering literature for what Eddy3D historically called "wind factors") is the local wind speed normalized by the reference speed.

docs.title: Boundary Conditions (BC)
docs.text: OutdoorBoundaryConditions object from the ABL or Uniform Flow component.

docs.title: Wind Velocity (U)
docs.text: DataTree of velocity vectors {direction}[probe] from the probe results.

docs.title: EPW
docs.text: Path to the EPW weather file.

docs.title: Height
docs.text: Probing/pedestrian height in meters.

docs.title: Interp
docs.text: Interpolate between bracketing wind directions.

docs.title: Wind
docs.text: Annual wind field object (every point's 8760-hour wind-speed series) for the Pedestrian Wind Comfort component. A single object rather than a data tree, so a few thousand probes stay fast.

docs.title: VAF
docs.text: DataTree {direction}[probe] of Velocity Amplification Factors (VAF) — local wind speed normalized by the reference (undisturbed) speed.

docs.title: ViewFactors - [source code]
docs.text: Configure the view-factor discretization for radiation modeling. OutdoorPlus

docs.title: SideFaces
docs.text: Number of faces in the coarsest level for domain side walls.

docs.title: TopFaces
docs.text: Number of faces in the coarsest level for the domain top.

docs.title: TerrainFaces
docs.text: Number of faces in the coarsest level for terrain.

docs.title: BuildingFaces
docs.text: Number of faces in the coarsest level for buildings.

docs.title: VegFaces
docs.text: Number of faces in the coarsest level for vegetation.

docs.title: ViewFactors
docs.text: View-factor settings.

docs.title: Viral Emitter - [source code]
docs.text: An airborne-pathogen passive-scalar source box for an indoor ventilation case.

docs.title: Zone (Z)
docs.text: Box zone occupied by the viral source.

docs.title: Injection Rate (IR)
docs.text: Viral tracer injection rate (specific).

docs.title: Source (S)
docs.text: Viral source for the Indoor Case component.

docs.title: Watertight - [source code]
docs.text: Combine a multi-part building mesh into a single watertight, CFD-ready solid via the bundled Python mesh service (trimesh/manifold3d/pymeshfix). The server auto-starts locally on the first run (uv-managed Python environment; first start installs it, 1-2 minutes) and is reused afterwards.

docs.title: Mesh (M)
docs.text: Input meshes (merged and combined into one solid).

docs.title: Min Length (L)
docs.text: Vertex weld tolerance in model units. Two vertices closer than this are merged. Default 0.1 (10 cm for meter-unit models).

docs.title: URL
docs.text: MetaBlock API base URL.

docs.title: Run
docs.text: Process the mesh — auto-starts the local server on first run. Resets when the result arrives.

docs.title: Combined (M)
docs.text: Combined watertight CFD-ready solid mesh.

docs.title: Status (S)
docs.text: Status message.

docs.title: Weather - [source code]
docs.text: Read an EPW file and create a Weather object for the simulation. OutdoorPlus

docs.title: Path
docs.text: Full path to the EPW weather file.

docs.title: Weather
docs.text: Weather object containing EPW data.

docs.title: Wind Comfort Predictor (ML) - [source code]
docs.text: Calculate Pedestrian Wind Comfort using predicted wind fields from the ONNX model.

docs.title: Pts
docs.text: Analysis points for mesh visualization.

docs.title: Wind Speeds (W)
docs.text: Predicted wind speeds (m/s) as a DataTree from WindPredictor.

docs.title: EPW
docs.text: Path to the .epw weather file.

docs.title: zRef
docs.text: Reference height for the simulations (m). Default = 10.0

docs.title: z0
docs.text: Roughness length (m). Default = 1.0

docs.title: Metric
docs.text: Comfort metric to use.

docs.title: Interp
docs.text: Interpolate between wind directions. Default = true

docs.title: Fast
docs.text: Use Method of Moments for ultra-fast Weibull estimation. Default = true

docs.title: Boundary Conditions (BC)
docs.text: Optional simulation metadata to automate zRef, z0, and Uref (sim).

docs.title: k
docs.text: Turbulent kinetic energy (m²/s²) as a DataTree from WindPredictor. When provided, GEM (Gust Equivalent Mean) is used: GEM = U + g × √(2k/3). Peak factor g is auto-set per metric.

docs.title: Wroof
docs.text: Optional roof-level wind speeds (m/s) DataTree.

docs.title: kroof
docs.text: Optional roof-level TKE (m²/s²) DataTree.

docs.title: Buildings
docs.text: Optional list of Brep or Mesh objects representing buildings. Needed to elevate roof-level comfort meshes to correct heights.

docs.title: Rank
docs.text: Wind comfort rank (integer).

docs.title: Letter
docs.text: Wind comfort class letter (e.g., A, B, C).

docs.title: Class
docs.text: Wind comfort class description.

docs.title: Comfort Mesh (M)
docs.text: Colored mesh representing comfort levels.

docs.title: Legend Mesh (LM)
docs.text: Legend mesh for comfort categories.

docs.title: Legend Points (LP)
docs.text: Label points for the legend letters.

docs.title: Legend Letters (LV)
docs.text: Letters (A-S) for the legend.

docs.title: MetricName
docs.text: The name of the currently active comfort/safety standard.

docs.title: Explanations
docs.text: Detailed descriptions for each class (e.g., A: Sitting Long).

docs.title: RankRoof
docs.text: Wind comfort rank (integer) for roof level.

docs.title: LetterRoof
docs.text: Wind comfort class letter for roof level.

docs.title: ClassRoof
docs.text: Wind comfort class description for roof level.

docs.title: Wind Compass - [source code]
docs.text: Visualize a wind direction on a compass circle. Direction is meteorological degrees (0=N, 90=E, 180=S, 270=W); outputs the flow vector and the 16-point cardinal name.

docs.title: Dir
docs.text: Wind direction in degrees (0=N, 90=E, 180=S, 270=W).

docs.title: Radius (R)
docs.text: Radius of the compass circle.

docs.title: Base Point (P)
docs.text: Center of the compass.

docs.title: Color (C)
docs.text: Display color.

docs.title: Arrow Scale (S)
docs.text: Scale of the directional arrow.

docs.title: Vec
docs.text: Wind direction (flow) vector.

docs.title: Name
docs.text: 16-point cardinal name (e.g. NNE).

docs.title: Wind Field Viewer - [source code]
docs.text: Visualize a probed wind field: colored velocity arrows, a point cloud, or a heatmap mesh. Feed the Probe component's points + velocity (or any points + vectors).

docs.title: Points (P)
docs.text: Sample points (e.g. the probe points).

docs.title: Velocity (V)
docs.text: Velocity vector per point.

docs.title: Display Mode (M)
docs.text: How to render the field: Vector Field (arrows), Point Cloud, Heatmap Mesh (colors a supplied mesh), Streamlines, or Volumetric Smoke.

docs.title: Msh
docs.text: Surface to color for Heatmap Mesh mode (colored per vertex from the nearest sample). Ignored in the other modes.

docs.title: Scale (S)
docs.text: Arrow length scale (Vector Field mode).

docs.title: Min
docs.text: Lower end of the color range / filter (m/s). Empty = data minimum.

docs.title: Max
docs.text: Upper end of the color range / filter (m/s). Empty = data maximum.

docs.title: Color Map (CM)
docs.text: Color ramp for the speed coloring.

docs.title: Geometry (G)
docs.text: Colored viz geometry for baking: arrow lines, points, or the colored mesh.

docs.title: Colors (C)
docs.text: Color per element (aligned with Geometry).

docs.title: Wind Predictor - [source code]
docs.text: Run ONNX wind-field prediction end-to-end. Computes SDF, building height, Zrelative, U/Uref, direction features from geometry, assembles the 8-channel input tensor, runs ONNX inference, and outputs predicted wind speeds. Supports legacy 1ch (U), 2ch (U + k) and new 4ch (U + k + Uroof + kroof) models.

docs.title: Points
docs.text: List of 3D points. Each point's Z must be absolute elevation (m).

docs.title: Buildings
docs.text: List of Brep or Mesh objects representing buildings.

docs.title: Model
docs.text: Full file path to the ONNX model. Connect the FilePath output from the ML Model component.

docs.title: Boundary Conditions (BC)
docs.text: Boundary conditions from the ABL or Uniform Flow component. Uref, zRef, z0, wind directions, and EPW path are extracted automatically.

docs.title: PedLevel
docs.text: Pedestrian mount height (m). Default = 1.8

docs.title: FiltMargin
docs.text: Margin (in meters) to mask out from the outer perimeter of the prediction plane due to unstable boundary effects. Default = 100.0

docs.title: Palette
docs.text: Color palette for visualization.

docs.title: Domain
docs.text: Optional custom domain [min, max] to lock the color bounds. If empty, the colors scale dynamically to the data.

docs.title: Interp
docs.text: Visualization style. Flat (pixelated) vs Smooth (interpolated colors).

docs.title: Field
docs.text: Field to visualize. Affects M, LM, LP, LV outputs.

docs.title: Level
docs.text: Visualization level. Roof Level only applies when a 4-channel model is loaded.

docs.title: X
docs.text: X coordinate (m) of each valid input point.

docs.title: Y
docs.text: Y coordinate (m) of each valid input point.

docs.title: Values (V)
docs.text: Predicted field values at each valid input point. Outputs wind speed (m/s) or turbulent kinetic energy (m²/s²) depending on the Field input. Branches represent different wind directions.

docs.title: Grid Mesh (M)
docs.text: A fast-rendering contiguous coloured preview mesh of the predictions.

docs.title: Legend Mesh (LM)
docs.text: A colored mesh strip acting as a visual legend.

docs.title: Legend Points (LP)
docs.text: Locations for the legend text in the 3D viewport (Generic type to prevent red cross preview).

docs.title: Legend Values (LV)
docs.text: Text values corresponding to the generated legend.

docs.title: Boundary Conditions (BC)
docs.text: Automated simulation boundary conditions metadata.

docs.title: Vroof
docs.text: Predicted roof-level field values at each valid input point. Outputs wind speed or TKE depending on the Field input. Branches represent different wind directions. Empty unless a 4-channel model is used.

docs.title: Wind Rose Cluster - [source code]
docs.text: Cluster annual wind directions into representative directions using k-means.

docs.title: Dirs
docs.text: Wind directions in degrees (e.g. hourly values from an EPW).

docs.title: Budget
docs.text: Maximum number of representative directions (clusters).

docs.title: Centroids
docs.text: Representative centroid direction of each cluster.

docs.title: Dirs
docs.text: Distinct representative wind directions in degrees, sorted ascending; plug into the ABL or Uniform Flow component's Wind Directions input.

docs.title: Clusters
docs.text: Clustered direction vectors as points, one branch per cluster.

docs.title: Breaks
docs.text: Jenks-Fisher natural breaks of the input directions.

docs.title: Error
docs.text: Total clustering distance (error).

docs.title: Write Run Scripts - [source code]
docs.text: Writes meshing and simulation scripts (.bat / .sh) into a Scripts/ folder under the wind study, so the workflow can be launched manually outside Grasshopper. The scripts match what the Run component executes. Write the study to disk first (Wind Case 'Write').

docs.title: Case (C)
docs.text: The wind study to generate run scripts for (Wind Case output).

docs.title: Parallel (P)
docs.text: Generate scripts that decompose and run in parallel (MPI).

docs.title: CPUs (N)
docs.text: Number of subdomains (MPI ranks) for parallel meshing and simulation. Leave unset (or <= 1) for automatic: the case's decomposeParDict count if > 1, else half the host cores.

docs.title: Engine (E)
docs.text: OpenFOAM engine the scripts target. BlueCFD/WSL produce .bat; Docker produces .sh.

docs.title: Probe
docs.text: Function-object / probes name the generated 05_PostProcess scripts sample. Match the Probe component's Name input. Default 'probes'.

docs.title: Write (W)
docs.text: Set to true to (re)write the scripts into the study's Scripts/ folder.

docs.title: Scripts Folder (F)
docs.text: Path to the Scripts/ folder containing the generated batch/shell files.

docs.title: Logs (L)
docs.text: Names of the scripts that were written.

docs.title: Zero Gradient Value - [source code]
docs.text: Create a zeroGradient Value.
